requirements.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list
Notes are displayed with their titles visible
User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes
User can access a search function
Search input field is visible or accessible
User can access an AI chat or assistant feature
Chat interface has an input field for questions
User can see a button or option to create a new note
New note creation is accessible from the main screen
Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy
Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes
Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note
Formatting toolbar or options are available
User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)
App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)
Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile
Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

requirement.description: User can view all notes in a hierarchical list

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a list of notes in a hierarchy on an iPhone, but the full content of each note is not visible due to a blurred view. Therefore, it's not possible to fully evaluate if the notes are being displayed in a hierarchical list.
results.notes: More information is needed to assess this requirement accurately.

requirement.description: Notes are displayed with their titles visible

notes.Suggestion: The image shows several notes with their titles visible. However, not all notes are fully visible or in clear focus. The UI could benefit from better contrast and better spacing between the notes to improve visibility and ease of use.

requirement.description: User can see a sidebar or navigation to browse notes

results.notes: Full view of navigation menu is not visible in this screenshot. It's unclear if there are more options or the ability to interact with them.

requirement.description: User can access a search function

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of an iOS app with various menu options at the bottom. However, there is no visible search icon or input field that would indicate the presence of a search function. Based on what's visible, it seems likely that this requirement may not be met.
results.notes: The screenshot does not provide enough information to determine if the user can access a search function in other parts of the app or if there are additional menu options available that would fulfill this requirement.

requirement.description: Search input field is visible or accessible

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a phone screen displaying a messaging application with a list of conversations visible, but the search input field is not immediately apparent or accessible. There's no input box that looks like a typical search field within the conversation view.
results.notes: To fully assess if this requirement is met, it would be helpful to have a clearer view of the messaging app or the context in which the user might use a search feature. Additionally, considering the nature of the messaging app and its functionality, it's possible that the search input field is accessible through other interfaces not visible in this screenshot.

requirement.description: User can access an AI chat or assistant feature

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a chat feature in the bottom left corner of the app screen, which could be related to an AI assistant.
results.notes: It is not clear if this is an AI chat or assistant feature. There is no visible text or icon indicating that it is an AI feature specifically. The presence of a keyboard suggests some form of interaction with the chat feature, but without more context or additional screenshots, I cannot definitively say whether it meets the requirement.

requirement.description: Chat interface has an input field for questions

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a chat interface with an input field for typing messages, but there is no visible input field specifically for questions. The input field is just the standard chat input area.
results.notes: It may be possible that the app has separate input fields for different types of chat entries (e.g., regular text and voice commands). However, from this screenshot alone, it's not clear if there are dedicated input fields for questions, which would need further investigation to determine if they exist elsewhere in the app.

requirement.description: User can see a button or option to create a new note

results.evidence: In the image, there is a button visible in the top right corner of the screen with a plus sign symbolizing the action to create a new note. This aligns with the requirement 'User can see a button or option to create a new note'.
results.notes: The layout and design of the application indicate that it is an iOS app, which should be taken into account when reviewing whether the button is standard for iOS devices.

requirement.description: New note creation is accessible from the main screen

results.evidence: In the screenshot, there is a note on the screen that says 'New note creation' is accessible from the main screen. However, it does not appear to be an active button or link for creating new notes.
results.notes: It may be necessary to review the app in use or check if there are other ways to create new notes that are not visible in this screenshot.

requirement.description: Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy

results.evidence: The image shows a keyboard on an iOS device with several keys on it, including those for numbers and letters. The requirement 'Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy' is related to the functionality of notes, not their visual display. Without any visible indication of the notes app within the screenshot provided, it is not possible to determine if this requirement is met based on this image alone.
results.notes: The UI elements in the screenshot do not provide enough information to assess whether the 'Notes appear to support nesting or hierarchy' requirement has been met. To make a proper assessment of the requirement, we would need additional context, such as interacting with the notes app or seeing different states and options within it.

requirement.description: Indentation or folder structure is visible for nested notes

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a nested notes section where each nesting level is indented. The first level of nesting starts with an arrow icon and the second level further indents.
results.notes: While there is only one level of nesting visible in this screenshot, the indentation is clear and the visual hierarchy is maintained.

requirement.description: Editor area is visible when viewing/editing a note

results.evidence: The image provided does not show an editor area visible when viewing or editing a note in the given app. The user interface presented is only a list of notes with no indication that there is an option to edit or add content within them.
results.notes: Based on this image, it seems that the block editor feature is either missing from this particular view or not enabled for certain users or actions.

requirement.description: Formatting toolbar or options are available

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile keyboard with an option 'Formatting toolbar or options are available'. However, it's not clear if this is the actual formatting toolbar or options for the block editor in question.
results.notes: It would be necessary to interact with the app and ensure that there is indeed a formatting toolbar or options within the block editor. The keyboard shortcut may suggest an alternative method of accessing these tools, but it's not clear if this is comprehensive.

requirement.description: User can see block-level content (paragraphs, headings, lists)

results.evidence: The image shows a screenshot of an app with a keyboard visible at the bottom. There are three blocks of content on the screen: a header, a paragraph, and a list. These blocks of content can be seen in their entirety, meeting the requirement for a user to see block-level content.

requirement.description: App has mobile-appropriate navigation (bottom tabs or hamburger menu)

results.evidence: In the provided screenshot, a mobile device is displaying an application interface. The UI includes bottom navigation tabs labeled 'MMD', 'ECOM', and 'TEST'. These bottom navigation tabs align with the requirement of having mobile-appropriate navigation in the form of bottom tabs. This indicates that the app has implemented a common mobile design pattern for easy user navigation.

requirement.description: Touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile

results.evidence: The screenshot shows a mobile device displaying an app interface. The touch targets, which appear to be buttons, are varying in size and do not seem to follow a consistent pattern or standard for touch target sizes. However, the context of the image does not provide information on whether these sizes are appropriate or not.
results.notes: While some touch targets are quite large, others are smaller, and there is no clear consistency in their design. To fully assess if the touch targets appear appropriately sized for mobile devices, additional information would be needed such as knowledge of standard touch target sizes for mobile interfaces.

requirement.description: Content is readable without horizontal scrolling

results.evidence: The image shows a mobile keyboard at the bottom of the screen, which is partially obscuring the view of the content on the display. This suggests that there may be horizontal scrolling required to read some parts of the content. However, it's not possible from this screenshot alone whether all content on the display is readable without horizontal scrolling.
results.notes: The requirement cannot be fully assessed due to the keyboard obscuring part of the display. To determine if the requirement is met, it would be necessary to view the app in a state where the keyboard is not visible or to show a different screen where no keyboard is present.
